case_name: Sangeeta Devi W/O Sh. Vinod Kumar Singh vs Mahesh Chand & Ors on 16 March, 2018
court: Motor Accident Claims Tribunal( Pilot Court) Karkardooma Courts, Delhi

compensation_breakdown.interest: 9% p.a. from date of filing of petition till realization
compensation_breakdown.notes: Award rounded to Rs. 5,74,000/-; directions for deposit and fixed deposit (FDR) scheme given.

facts: On 14.01.2014 at about 3-3:40 PM near Annapurna Mandir, Sonia Vihar, Delhi a motorcycle bearing No. DL 3 SBY 5198 driven by respondent No.1 allegedly drove rashly and negligently and struck the claimant's son Shivam. Shivam sustained grievous injuries, was taken to Jag Pravesh Chandra Hospital and referred to Lok Nayak Hospital where he died during course of treatment on 14.01.2014; post-mortem was conducted at Maulana Azad Medical College & Lok Nayak Hospital. The claimant (mother) filed a DAR/claim petition under the Motor Vehicles Act seeking compensation; respondent did not file a substantive reply or lead evidence to rebut negligence.
arguments_claimant: The claimant relied on FIR, MLC and criminal records and oral evidence (PW-1) to establish that the motorcycle driven by respondent caused the fatal accident by rash and negligent driving and sought compensation under the Motor Vehicles Act. The claimant argued entitlement to pecuniary and non-pecuniary damages and applied the Delhi High Court formula for computing compensation in child death cases.
arguments_respondent: Respondent did not file a substantive reply or lead evidence; the record notes denial only and no witnesses to rebut the claimant's case. The tribunal records that respondent argued (contention noted in judgment) that petitioner had not suffered monetary loss, but offered no documentary proof to contradict accident or cause.
ratio_decidendi: "Subject to all other requisite conditions being fulfilled, for the foregoing reasons, in order to bring about consistency and uniformity in approach to the issue, it is held that claims for compensation on account of death of children shall be determined as follows: (i) ... the notional income of non-earning persons (Rs. 15,000/- p.a.) as specified in the second schedule ... shall be assumed to be the income of the deceased child, and taken into account after it is inflation corrected with the help of Cost inflation Index (CII) ...; (ii) For inflation correction, the financial year of 1997-1998 shall be treated as the 'base year' ... Rs. 15,000/- x A/331 ...; (iv) The amount of notional income thus calculated shall be reduced to two-third, the deduction to the extent of one third being towards personal & living expenses of the deceased; (v) For assessment of the pecuniary damages on account of the death of children up to the age of 10 years, the loss to estate shall be calculated, capitalizing the multiplicand, by applying the multiplier of ten (10)." The tribunal applied this Delhi High Court formula to the facts (age ~10 years, CII for 2013-14 = 939) and computed inflation-corrected notional income, reduced one-third for personal expenses, applied multiplier 10, and then added an equal amount as composite non-pecuniary damages to arrive at the final award.
final_order: DAR petition allowed. Respondent directed to pay Rs. 5,74,000/- to the claimant within one month with interest @ 9% p.a. from date of filing of petition till realization; respondent directed to deposit award amount in court and Rs.5,00,000/- to be kept in 100 FDRs of Rs.5,000/- each as directed.
summary: This is a Motor Accident Claim (DAR No.14937/15) filed by the mother of a child (Shivam) who died when hit by a motorcycle in Sonia Vihar on 14.01.2014. The Tribunal found the respondent-driver rash and negligent (claimant's FIR/MLC/criminal record and unrebutted testimony accepted) and applied the Delhi High Court formula for compensation in child death cases, calculating inflation-adjusted notional income, deducting one-third and applying multiplier 10. The Tribunal awarded Rs.5,74,000/- with interest at 9% p.a. and directed deposit and FDR arrangements for the award funds.
embedding_summary: Sangeeta Devi v. Mahesh Chand, DAR No.14937/15, Motor Accident Claims Tribunal, Karkardooma Courts (2018). Accident on 14.01.2014 near Annapurna Mandir, Sonia Vihar: motorcycle No. DL 3 SBY 5198 driven by respondent struck child Shivam (age ~10) who died at Lok Nayak Hospital same day. Issues: negligence of driver, entitlement to compensation under Motor Vehicles Act (s.158(6) treated as claim), and computation of compensation for death of a child. Tribunal applied Delhi High Court formula (use Rs.15,000 notional income, CII inflation correction with base year 1997-98, reduce one-third for personal expenses, apply multiplier 10 for children up to 10 years, add composite non-pecuniary equal to pecuniary) and awarded Rs.5,74,000/- with interest @9% p.a.; directions given for deposit and FDRs. Key authorities and statutes cited include R.K. Malik, Sarla Verma, Nagappa, Bimla Devi, Kaushnumma Begum, Delhi High Court MAC Apps No.554/2010 decision on child compensation, and references to Section 158(6), Section 163-A(3) M.V. Act and CII under Section 48 Income Tax Act.
legal_principles: Delhi High Court formula for death of children: assume Rs.15,000/- notional income, inflation-correct by CII (base year 1997-98), reduce one-third for personal expenses, apply age-based multiplier (10 for up to 10 years), and add composite non-pecuniary equal to pecuniary
Where FIR/charge-sheet/MLC and criminal record are produced and respondent offers no effective rebuttal, negligence can be presumed on preponderance of probabilities
Claims under Motor Vehicles Act are benevolent and assessed on preponderance of probabilities, not strict criminal standard
Compensation should be 'just and reasonable' and consistent with established multipliers and precedents (R.K. Malik, Sarla Verma)
Award attracts interest (here 9% p.a.) from date of filing until realization
cases_cited: Bimla Devi and Ors. v. Himachal Road Transport Corporation and Ors., (2009) 13 SC 530
United India Insurance Company Ltd. Vs. Deepak Goel & Ors., 2014 (2), T.A.C. 846 (Del.)
National Insurance Company Limited Vs. Pushpa Rana, 2009 ACJ 287 (Delhi High Court)
Kaushnumma Begum and others v. New India Assurance Company Limited, 2001 ACJ 421 SC
UP State Road Transport Corporation v. Trilok Chandra, (1996) 4 SCC 362
General Manager Kerala State Road Transport Corporation v. Susamma Thomas, (1994) 2 SCC 176
R. K. Malik v. Kiran Pal, 2009 (14) SCC 1
Delhi High Court MAC Apps No. 554/2010 & Conn. (decision dated 13.05.2016) (formula for child death compensation)
New India Assurance Co. Ltd. Vs. Gopali & Ors., 2012 ACJ 2131 SC
Jiju Kuruvila & Ors. Vs. Kunjujamma Mohan & Ors., 2013 ACJ 2141 SC
Puttamma Vs. K.L. Naraynan Reddy & Ors., 2014 ACJ 526 SC

Shivam sustained grievous injuries, was taken to Jag Pravesh Chandra Hospital and referred to Lok Nayak Hospital where he died during course of treatment on 14.01.2014; post-mortem was conducted at Maulana Azad Medical College & Lok Nayak Hospital. The claimant (mother) filed a DAR/claim petition under the Motor Vehicles Act seeking compensation; respondent did not file a substantive reply or lead evidence to rebut negligence. The claimant relied on FIR, MLC and criminal records and oral evidence (PW-1) to establish that the motorcycle driven by respondent caused the fatal accident by rash and negligent driving and sought compensation under the Motor Vehicles Act. The claimant argued entitlement to pecuniary and non-pecuniary damages and applied the Delhi High Court formula for computing compensation in child death cases. Respondent did not file a substantive reply or lead evidence; the record notes denial only and no witnesses to rebut the claimant's case. The tribunal records that respondent argued (contention noted in judgment) that petitioner had not suffered monetary loss, but offered no documentary proof to contradict accident or cause. "Subject to all other requisite conditions being fulfilled, for the foregoing reasons, in order to bring about consistency and uniformity in approach to the issue, it is held that claims for compensation on account of death of children shall be determined as follows: (i) ... the notional income of non-earning persons (Rs. 15,000/- p.a.) as specified in the second schedule ... shall be assumed to be the income of the deceased child, and taken into account after it is inflation corrected with the help of Cost inflation Index (CII) ...; (ii) For inflation correction, the financial year of 1997-1998 shall be treated as the 'base year' ... Rs. 15,000/- x A/331 ...; (iv) The amount of notional income thus calculated shall be reduced to two-third, the deduction to the extent of one third being towards personal & living expenses of the deceased; (v) For assessment of the pecuniary damages on account of the death of children up to the age of 10 years, the loss to estate shall be calculated, capitalizing the multiplicand, by applying the multiplier of ten (10)." The tribunal applied this Delhi High Court formula to the facts (age ~10 years, CII for 2013-14 = 939) and computed inflation-corrected notional income, reduced one-third for personal expenses, applied multiplier 10, and then added an equal amount as composite non-pecuniary damages to arrive at the final award. DAR petition allowed. Respondent directed to pay Rs. 5,74,000/- to the claimant within one month with interest @ 9% p.a. from date of filing of petition till realization; respondent directed to deposit award amount in court and Rs.5,00,000/- to be kept in 100 FDRs of Rs.5,000/- each as directed. The Tribunal found the respondent-driver rash and negligent (claimant's FIR/MLC/criminal record and unrebutted testimony accepted) and applied the Delhi High Court formula for compensation in child death cases, calculating inflation-adjusted notional income, deducting one-third and applying multiplier 10.
